title: Loupe Browser Access and Loading Data
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"blfvf","text":"Information for accessing Loupe Browser, particularly for URMC users on Momentum.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Loupe Browser is a tool provided by 10X Genomics for analysis of Chromium and Visium/Visium HD datasets. It is provided as a free download at https://www.10xgenomics.com/support/software/loupe-browser/downloads . For non-URMC users, downloading the software will be the only way to use Loupe Browser. For URMC users, access to this software is provided through the Office of Research IT (ORIT).
Momentum is a platform used to access university hosted softwares made available by ORIT. Upon reasonable request, ORIT is able to host free software for deployment via remote resources.

After logging into Momentum, click on the tile for Loupe Browser. You can also use the search bar to locate it. Click the "Connect from Browser" option. The "Connect from Computer" option can be used as a backup option, but we have found it to be less stable than browser access.
Log into Momentum via https://momentum.urmc-sh.rochester.edu/momentum , using your AD login.

If connecting via browser, you will be taken to an Apache Guacamole portal. Log in using your AD credentials. You will then need to perform a duo authentication and then you should have access to the browser. If connecting via computer, a remote desktop file will be downloaded. When launching this file, you will be prompted for duo authentication and a desktop session will be configured. This may take some time.
Once the Loupe Browser window opens, click the 'Open Loupe File' button.

Enter the path to the data you wish to load in Loupe Browser. The file type that Loupe Browser needs is a cloupe file. You can find this file in the delivery directory associated with your project. You can search for this file using the file explorer address bar. When accessing Loupe Browser from Momentum, you can navigate to your smdnas02 share by typing '\\smdnas02\GRC_' in the address bar, and then select the appropriate share and project folder. Finally, double click the cloupe file to open it.

For Visium data processed with Space Ranger v4 and above, you will have cloupe files associated with both the binned outputs as well as the cell segmentation outputs . There are cloupe files associated with each of these outputs, which you can open and explore in Loupe Browser. The cloupe file associated with the binned outputs can be found here: deliv_*_counts/Sample_ID/outs/binned_outputs/square_*um/ The cloupe file associated with the cell segmented outputs can be found here: deliv_*_counts/Sample_ID/outs/segmented_outputs/ Additional details on understanding Space Ranger Outputs can be found on 10X's website here: https://www.10xgenomics.com/support/software/space-ranger/latest/analysis/outputs/output-overview
creator: Hannah Aichelman
